content.full_text:
March 22, 1989 
MEMO TO FILE 
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 
Mary Ann Kvasager 
RE: 
Telephone Advice Provided to Barbara Rhomberg, 
City of Oakland 
T89-1~3 
The attached letter dated February 15, 1989 from Barbara Rhomberg, 
Administrative Assistant, City of Oakland, correctly summarizes 
the telephone advice I provided to her. 
Ms. Rhomberg did not 
request written advice. 


A 
February 15, 1989 
Ms. Marianne Kvaseger 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Marianne, 
Thank you for answering my questions regarding the campaign 
contribution limits for partnerships and partners under Proposition 
73. 
Despite your clear exposition, I'm anxious to verify that I 
understood the limits correctly. 
This letter will confirm our 
conversation. 
As I understood it, there are three possible situations regarding 
contributions from partnerships: 
1. If one partner owns 50% or more of the firm, then any 
contribution made by the firm will count against that partner's 
limit. 
In other words, the sum of the majority partner's 
personal contribution and the firm's contribution may not exceed 
$1000. 
2. If the partner owns less than 50% of the firm, and does not 
direct the firm's contribution, then the partner may contribute 
up to $1000 from personal funds without regard to the 
contribution by the firm. (The firm is also limited to $1000.) 
3. If the partner owns less than 50% of the firm, but does direct 
the firm's donation, then -- as in the first case -- the total of 
the partner's personal contribution and the firm's contribution 
may not exceed $1000. 
I expect no answer to this letter unless my understanding is 
erroneous. 
Again, I appreciate your help on this aspect of the new 
contribution limits. 
Barbara Rhomberg 
Administrative Assi 




California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Barbara Rhomberg 
Administrative Assistant 
City of Oakland 
City Hall 
One city Hall Plaza 
Oakland, CA 
94612 
February 27, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-123 
Dear Ms. Rhomberg: 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the Political Reform Act on February 22, 1989. 
Your letter 
has been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division or response. 
If you have any questions, you may contact 
them directly at (916) 322-5662. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh:confadv1 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
l t t,-
428 J Street. Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660

sections.question_synthetic: How do campaign contribution limits under Proposition 73 apply to partnerships and partners in three different ownership scenarios (majority owner, minority owner not directing contribution, and minority owner directing contribution)?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains multiple letters: (1) memo to file from FPPC staff, (2) incoming request letter from Barbara Rhomberg seeking confirmation of telephone advice on Proposition 73 partnership contribution limits, and (3) FPPC acknowledgment/routing letter. This appears to be an incoming request rather than a formal FPPC advice response. The memo indicates no written advice was requested. No formal conclusion section present.

embedding.summary: This document contains a memo from FPPC staff Mary Ann Kvasager documenting telephone advice provided to Barbara Rhomberg of Oakland regarding partnership contribution limits, along with Rhomberg's confirmation letter and FPPC's acknowledgment letter assigning the matter for response. No formal written conclusion was provided in the materials presented.
